title: centrolepis mutica
intro: Centrolepis mutica, also known as New Zealand wind grass, is a small, perennial plant native to New Zealand and other parts of the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its essential oils and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Centrolepis mutica include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it a potential natural remedy for wound care and skin issues. Herbal preparations of Centrolepis mutica can be made into teas, infusions, and extracts, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
